steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the text. The passage introduces habitat navigation as a skill, then describes an experiment where goldfish were taught to drive a motorized tank with wheels — navigating on land. The main idea must capture both the surprising finding and its significance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Fish can navigate only within their natural aquatic environment in order to find food and safety.: This is the opposite of what the experiment shows — the goldfish successfully navigated outside their aquatic environment. ✗
  • Choice B — An animal's navigational skills directly depend on that animal's particular environment.: The experiment actually challenges this idea by showing a fish navigating a land environment. The passage doesn't support environment as a hard requirement. ✗
  • Choice C — Researchers can train animals to complete many different tasks when they offer the animals treats.: No treats are mentioned in the passage. This misrepresents the experiment. ✗
  • Choice D — Research shows that although they live in the water, fish can navigate on land with a little mechanical help.: Captures the core finding: fish (a water animal) navigated outside their habitat using a mechanical vehicle. ✓
D is correct.
